title: From Heaven Above To Earth I Come
words: Martin Luther, 1535. translated by Catherine Winkworth, 1855
music: traditional German from Schumann's Geistliche Lieder, Leipzig, 1539

lyrics.v1: From Heaven above to earth I come, To bear good news to every home; Glad tidings of great joy I bring, Whereof I now will say and sing.
lyrics.v2: To you, this night, is born a Child Of Mary, chosen mother mild; This tender Child of lowly birth, Shall be the joy of all your earth.
lyrics.v3: 'Tis Christ our God, Who far on high Had heard your sad and bitter cry; Himself will your Salvation be, Himself from sin will make you free.
lyrics.v4: He brings those blessings long ago Prepared by God for all below; That in His heavenly kingdom blest You may with us forever rest.
lyrics.v5: These are the tokens ye shall mark, The swaddling clothes and manger dark; There shall ye find the young Child laid, By Whom the heav'ns and earth were made.

lyrics.v1: From Heaven above to earth I come,
lyrics.v2: To you, this night, is born a Child

lyrics.v1: To bear good news to every home;
